Incumbent Wesley Hunt vacated the seat for a U.S. Senate bid, but the March 3 primaries solidified Democrat Melissa McDonough as nominee after her 51.6% win, while Republicans head to a May 26 runoff between Trump-endorsed Jon Bonck (47.7% in primary) and Shelly deZevallos. GOP fundraising dominance—over $2 million combined versus McDonough's $48,000—bolsters the edge ahead of the November general election.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as the leading candidate at 61.4% to remain Venezuela's official head of state by December 31, 2026, reflecting persistent recognition by state institutions as constitutional president despite his January 3 capture by U.S. forces and ongoing detention. Delcy Rodríguez, his former vice president now serving as acting president, holds 27.5% implied probability amid her recent power consolidation, including U.S. sanction relief in early April, a meeting with Colombia's leader on April 24, and prisoner releases criticized by rights groups. Opposition figures like María Corina Machado at 7.5% gain limited traction as demands for presidential elections—post the expired 90-day interim period—remain unmet, underscoring institutional continuity and transition uncertainty under external pressures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Trump-Xi Jinping meeting at 99% probability, driven by the White House confirmation of a May 14-15 Beijing summit to address the ongoing Iran war, trade tensions, Taiwan, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions—delayed from March amid U.S. military escalations. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in South African Weather Service (SAWS) forecasts for Cape Town's May 13 maximum temperature, with 16–19°C outcomes clustered at 18–22% implied probabilities amid lingering effects of an intense cold front that brought frigid, wet, and windy conditions through May 10–12. Recent SAWS updates project highs near 17–18°C on May 12, with isolated showers and cloudy skies likely persisting into May 13, moderating temperatures below the mid-May climatological average of 19°C due to post-frontal cooling, persistent south-easterly winds channeling cool Benguela Current air, and variable low-level cloud. Ensemble model spreads from ECMWF and GFS show 2–3°C divergence tied to clearing timing and orographic influences from Table Mountain; watch SAWS' daily briefing and new model runs on May 12 for potential shifts.

markets.question: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/6b1814d64903461b98144a6cc25eb79c.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Bab el-Mandeb Strait closure reflects heightened geopolitical risks from Houthi threats issued in April 2026, amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade since March that propelled Brent crude above $120 per barrel. The chokepoint, vital for 12% of global oil trade and 30% of container shipping, sees transits collapsed to 10-15 vessels daily versus pre-crisis 130-150, driving Asia-Europe freight rates up 300-500% via Cape detours and war-risk insurance premiums to $3-3.6 million annually per vessel. US-led coalition patrols provide deterrence, tempering full-blockade fears, while demurrage accrues on 230+ stranded tankers. Key catalysts include potential Houthi strikes and US retaliatory actions.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

Nikolai Denkov (11.5%) and Sofia Mayor Vassil Terziev (9.8%), both from the centrist We Continue the Change-Democratic Bulgaria alliance that garnered just 12-13% parliamentarily, trail amid fragmented opposition, while GERB's Rosen Zhelyazkov (4.5%) reflects weaker center-right positioning. The two-round system favors frontrunners, but opposition consolidation could challenge in a potential runoff.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?

The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

markets.description:

If this tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a winner has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizer, ESL (https://pro.eslgaming.com/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality leads trader consensus at 60.5% implied probability to win IEM Cologne Major 2026, fueled by their unmatched 2026 dominance including recent BLAST Rivals Spring and IEM Rio titles—sweeping grand finals 3-0 over NAVI and Spirit—plus an ongoing map win-streak extending into IEM Atlanta this week where they hold the #1 seed. As HLTV #1 with a direct Stage 3 invite, their stacked roster featuring ZywOo and ropz embodies peak form ahead of the Swiss stages starting June 2. Falcons (10%) trail as HLTV #5 with Stage 3 seeding, bolstered by competitive head-to-heads against Vitality, while Spirit (7%) and NAVI (4.9%) reflect solid top-6 rankings but recent grand final losses. Rising squads like PARIVISION (3.3%, #7) and FUT (3%) earned strong invites via Valve standings, positioning them as dark horses in the $1.25M event's single-elim playoffs.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: United Russia's entrenched dominance as the ruling party, backed by President Putin and vast administrative resources, drives its 96% implied probability on Polymarket to claim the most seats in the State Duma elections scheduled for September 18–20. Late April polls from FOM and WCIOM show party-list support at 27–39%, well ahead of rivals like LDPR (10%), KPRF (9%), and New People (6–13%), with half the 450 seats decided by proportional lists and half by single-member constituencies where United Russia historically excels. Kremlin preparations feature electronic primaries, a top list led by Dmitry Medvedev and war veterans, and redistricting adding seats in occupied regions. Opposition parties face internal crises and suppression, limiting threats. Scenarios like a severe economic downturn or battlefield reversals in Ukraine could erode this edge, though controls make upsets improbable.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Pam Bondi's abrupt dismissal as Attorney General in early April, President Trump elevated Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche—his former personal defense lawyer—to acting AG, with no permanent nominee announced in the six weeks since, driving trader consensus to price No Announcement by June 30 at 50%. Mid-April rumors of EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin as frontrunner, who dodged related questions, elevated his implied probability to 12.5%, while Blanche's 10% reflects his interim stability and legal analyses suggesting he could serve through Trump's term without Senate confirmation under the Vacancies Reform Act. Lower odds for Harmeet Dhillon (8%) and Texas AG Ken Paxton (7.4%) stem from their Trump ally status amid ongoing DOJ probes and Paxton's Texas Senate primary distractions, underscoring delays in the nomination process.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Haaland bolstered his advantage in Manchester City's 3-0 home win over Brentford on May 9, extending his prolific form amid City's title push, while Thiago blanked despite recent strikes like his goal against West Ham on May 2. No injury concerns plague Haaland, whose clinical finishing and City support solidify trader consensus. Realistic challenges include a Haaland injury, suspension, or rest if the title is secured, paired with Thiago netting five-plus goals in Brentford's final fixtures against mid-table sides.

markets.question: Will Melania Trump attend Trump’s Xi summit?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to visit China for a summit with Xi Jinping around May 14-15, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends any portion of this summit. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present at any meeting or event which is part of this summit and for which officials from both the government of the United States and the government of China are present.

If no summit occurs by May 20, this market will resolve to "No". 

The summit will be considered to have ended once Donald Trump physically departs from Chinese territory. No meetings or events which start after this time will be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and China; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions the United Kingdom's Look Mum No Computer ("Eins, Zwei, Drei") and host nation Austria's COSMÓ ("Tanzschein") as near-dead heats for last place in the Eurovision 2026 grand final, with implied probabilities of 32% and 30.5%, driven by backlash to first rehearsal clips released over the past week. The UK's techno-gimmick staging—featuring a mad-scientist setup with furry-headed dancers—has reignited fears of zero televote points, echoing its dismal 2025 showing, while Austria's funky German-language track lacks the host-nation momentum needed amid stiff competition. Czechia ("Crossroads") and Germany ("Fire") trail as perceived weak links post-national finals. With semis kicking off May 12 and the final on May 16, live execution and jury-televote splits remain pivotal swing factors in this tight race.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 24°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's landslide endorsement at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, capturing 70% of delegates and surpassing the 15% threshold for ballot access, has driven trader consensus to price him as the overwhelming favorite in the September 1 Republican gubernatorial primary. Mike Kennealy's immediate campaign suspension after failing that threshold eliminated a key rival, narrowing the field to Minogue and Brian Shortsleeve, who recently secured his own ballot spot. Recent calls from state RNC members for Shortsleeve to exit and unite behind Minogue, amid Minogue's financial edge and internal polling leads, reflect party momentum favoring consolidation, though Shortsleeve presses for debates to appeal to primary voters.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, National Assembly elections under proportional representation, no party secured a majority in the 90-seat chamber, with Robert Golob's liberal Freedom Movement (GS) taking 29 seats and Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28, trailed by NSi-SLS-Fokus (9), Democrats (6), Social Democrats (6), and Resni.ca (5). Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed on April 20, prompting his party to enter opposition, while President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate on April 25 due to insufficient support for any candidate reaching 46 votes. SDS-led talks with NSi-SLS-Fokus, Democrats, and Resni.ca stalled by May 6 amid Resni.ca's refusal to back either major leader, raising snap election risks if parliament fails to elect a PM nominee soon. Trader consensus reflects this hung parliament uncertainty, hinging on coalition breakthroughs or dissolution.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 73.5% for a U.S. AI safety bill enacting before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress amid the Trump administration's deregulatory push. The White House's March 20 National Policy Framework emphasized light-touch sector-specific rules, federal preemption of state laws, and no new regulatory bodies, signaling limited appetite for comprehensive AI safety mandates. Despite introductions like the Ensuring Safe AI Act and child-focused bills advancing in committees, none have reached floor votes or presidential signature in the 119th Congress, now in its final months. State-level enactments, such as Nebraska's Conversational AI Safety Act, fill gaps but do not qualify as federal action, underscoring congressional gridlock and competing priorities ahead of lame-duck sessions.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service forecasts and NOAA ensemble model guidance project Chicago's highest temperature on May 12 at 68–72°F at O'Hare International Airport, the market's resolution site, driving the 99.5% implied probability for 64°F or higher. After an unseasonably cool early May with highs in the 50s, a warming ridge aloft and southwesterly winds are advecting milder air from the south, with model clusters showing minimal spread and little support for sub-64°F outcomes. This skin-in-the-game trader consensus aligns with historical May norms around 69°F and low volatility in short-range guidance. Realistic challenges include a sudden cool front or persistent thunderstorms capping highs, though current upper-air patterns and Geopotential Height analyses indicate low risk; monitor afternoon NWS updates for refinements.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
markets.description:
If Xi Jinping visits the United States between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 72% implied probability for Chinese President Xi Jinping to conduct a state visit to the United States before 2027, driven by reciprocal invitations exchanged between Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping. Following their October 2025 Busan summit and Trump's acceptance of a Beijing invitation, Trump confirmed Xi's planned US visit later in 2026—potentially November—signaling stabilized bilateral diplomacy on trade, Taiwan, and Iran tensions. With Trump's state visit to Beijing confirmed for May 14-15, traders price in high odds of follow-through summits amid ongoing foreign policy dialogues, though geopolitical flashpoints like tariffs or territorial disputes could still derail plans before year-end.

markets.question: Pigato vs. Bronzetti: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lisa Pigato and Lucia Bronzetti in the Parma, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Parma: Lisa Pigato vs Lucia Bronzetti Match O/U 22.5

events.title: Parma: Lisa Pigato vs Lucia Bronzetti
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lisa Pigato and Lucia Bronzetti in the Parma, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Lisa Pigato' if Lisa Pigato advances against Lucia Bronzetti.

This market will resolve to 'Lucia Bronzetti' if Lucia Bronzetti advances against Lisa Pigato.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the WTA 125 Parma Ladies Open first-round clash on clay, higher-ranked Italian Lisa Pigato (No. 141, 27-7 YTD) faces compatriot Lucia Bronzetti (No. 173, 8-12) in an all-azzurri derby boosted by recent wildcards. Pigato enters with momentum from clay challenger deep runs, including a QF win over Lina Gjorcheska last month, and holds a 1-0 H2H edge from their 2019 ITF clay meeting. Bronzetti, a surface specialist rebuilding after a rankings slide from peak No. 46, advanced in Oeiras but fell early in Rome to McCartney Kessler. No injuries reported; home crowd and rest from recent clay action could sway this competitive matchup.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent reacceleration in US inflation metrics is anchoring trader sentiment, with March 2026 CPI at 3.3% year-over-year—up from 2.4% prior—and April forecast at 3.7%, driven by surging energy prices from Middle East tensions. Core CPI rose 0.2% monthly, while PCE headline hit 3.5% annually, exceeding Fed comfort amid sticky services inflation. FOMC's March dot plot targets 2.7% PCE for full-year 2026, but officials now emphasize upside risks, with 5-year breakeven inflation at 2.69%. Tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12, alongside June FOMC, will be key catalysts shaping market-implied peak inflation odds for the year.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the best Math AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Math" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Math" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/math-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's recent Claude Mythos Preview release has driven its 51.5% implied probability as the leading math AI model by May 31, topping benchmarks like MATH-500 and BRUMO while showcasing superior reasoning on competition-level problems such as AIME variants. Traders view this as a breakout in Anthropic's large language model capabilities, building on Opus 4.7's strong showings and outpacing OpenAI's GPT-5.x series (35.5%), which dominates AIME at near-100% but trails in select evals. Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro (12.5%) lags amid weaker recent math gains. With resolution imminent, focus remains on full model deployments and independent verifications amid rapid AI benchmark shifts.

markets.question: Will Abdul El-Sayed win the 2026 Michigan Democratic Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Abdul El-Sayed leads trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary on August 4, driven by his dominance among voters under 40 in an April Data for Progress poll (41% support) and strong first-quarter fundraising outpacing Rep. Haley Stevens, per reports released April 28. Mallory McMorrow holds 28.5% on similar fundraising momentum and an Emerson College poll tie with El-Sayed overall. Stevens trails at 18.5% despite a narrow April Detroit Regional Chamber poll edge, boosted recently by ex-Sen. Debbie Stabenow's endorsement and a $5 million pro-Stevens ad buy from a mystery group. High undecideds (36% in latest polls) and progressive turnout questions keep the open-seat race fluid post-Sen. Gary Peters' retirement.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding 60%-39% victory over Republican Joe Hathaway in the April 16 NJ-11 special election, confirmed in final unofficial tallies reported last week, underpins trader consensus pricing her margin at 20-25%. This 21-point edge exceeds 2024 presidential results in the district (Harris +9) by 12 points, fueled by robust Democratic turnout in this safely blue suburban seat vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill, alongside Mejia's strong mobilization of progressive and working-class voters despite pockets of resistance in areas like Livingston. Official certification by county boards and the state Division of Elections remains pending, but with over 135,000 votes cast and no close margin triggering recount, significant shifts from provisional or late mail ballots are improbable absent verified irregularities.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Trump administration's abrupt May 2 announcement to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months—amid tensions with allies denying base access for US operations—has intensified scrutiny of America's NATO commitments, prompting NATO officials to seek clarification on impacted operations. However, a full US withdrawal remains constrained by Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, mandating congressional approval that the president cannot unilaterally override, as affirmed by bipartisan legislation. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has emphasized this troop repositioning does not equate to quitting the alliance. Trader sentiment reflects entrenched institutional inertia, historical US reluctance to exit despite past rhetoric, and geopolitical pressures from Russia, with upcoming NATO summits and Senate oversight hearings poised to influence diplomatic postures.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wellington be 17°C on May 12?

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

The contest remains tight in Romania's fragmented parliament, where coalition negotiations or PSD-PNL realignment could secure a majority; failure risks a second failed vote, snap elections, or technocratic appointment before late May deadlines.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

Recent polls show Rassemblement National's Jordan Bardella leading the first round at 34-38%, ahead of Horizons' Édouard Philippe (18-25%) and centrists like Gabriel Attal, reflecting RN gains post-municipal elections. Jean-Luc Mélenchon announced his fourth La France Insoumise bid on May 3, complicating left-wing unity primary plans for October 11, while conservatives selected Bruno Retailleau in April. Key upcoming: Marine Le Pen's July 7 embezzlement appeal, potentially paving Bardella's path if she is barred.

markets.question: Will Annie January (Starlight) die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 36.3% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the Central Bank's latest REM survey median of 30.5% end-year, up 1.4 percentage points from prior estimates. This shift stems from March 2026 CPI rising 3.4% month-over-month—above consensus—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4% amid seasonal education and transport pressures, despite President Milei's austerity measures curbing annual rates to 32.6% from February's 33.2%. Lower bins like 25-29.9% (24.5%) reflect optimism for further fiscal tightening, while upside risks above 40% (24.2% combined) account for potential policy slippage; April CPI data, due imminently, looms as the next catalyst.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

No next meeting is scheduled, though trader consensus eyes potential resumption in Pakistan; US-China summit May 14-15 elevates Iran on the agenda via recent Beijing talks with Iranian officials.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Colombia President Gustavo Petro at 40% to be the next leader out of power before 2027, driven by his constitutionally mandated term end on August 7 amid persistent low approval ratings around 60%, stalled reforms, and a polarized legislative landscape following March's left-leaning vote outcomes. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer trails closely at 31% after Labour's devastating local election losses last week, where the party shed hundreds of council seats to Reform UK, sparking senior MP calls for his resignation within months and polls showing majority public support for his exit. The tight race hinges on Starmer facing a no-confidence vote or internal revolt versus Petro's scheduled handover after May's presidential election; escalation in either could swiftly separate the frontrunners.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs Should I Marry A Murderer? as the #2 global Netflix TV show this week, with 92.5% implied probability, reflecting its dominant mid-week positioning in official Netflix Top 10 charts at #2 with 10.6 million views—just behind Man on Fire's 11 million and well ahead of Unchosen's 8.8 million at #3. This true crime limited series has sustained massive momentum since its recent debut, fueled by viral buzz as one of 2026's breakout documentaries, capturing audiences with its emotional true-story hooks amid a competitive field of new releases like Legends and The Boroughs. While streaming metrics can shift rapidly, an upset would require Unchosen or a late climber like Man on Fire challengers to surge over 1.8 million views in the final days before Tuesday's weekly chart lock, a tall order given current trajectories and historical patterns where early leads hold firm.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures (GC) for June delivery trade around $4,740 per ounce, reflecting trader consensus on persistent safe-haven demand amid U.S.-Iran tensions and a softer U.S. dollar index near 97.90, despite 10-year Treasury yields climbing to 4.39%. eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures surged above $97 per barrel on May 11 amid escalating US-Iran tensions, as President Trump's rejection of Tehran's peace proposal response reignited Strait of Hormuz transit risks and bolstered the geopolitical risk premium. US inventories drew down 2.3 million barrels to 457 million in the week ended May 1 per latest EIA data, slightly above five-year averages, supporting near-term firmness despite OPEC+ plans for modest June output hikes among seven key members following UAE's exit. Divergent forecasts—EIA projecting Brent peaks in 2Q26 before falling to $76/bbl in 2027, versus Goldman Sachs' $83 WTI Q4 call—highlight uncertainty, with traders monitoring weekly EIA releases and the June 7 OPEC+ ministerial meeting as pivotal ahead of end-June settlement.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

April McClain Delaney's sustained double-digit lead in recent Democratic primary polls—49% to David Trone's 38% in an April survey—bolsters trader consensus on a Democratic hold in Maryland's 6th Congressional District, rated Safe Democratic by Sabato's Crystal Ball and Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report. The district's Montgomery County Democratic suburbs offset western Maryland's GOP lean, where Delaney narrowly prevailed in her 2024 open-seat win. Weak Republican primary contenders like Robin Ficker trail in fundraising, limiting general election threats ahead of the June 23 primaries. Escalating primary rhetoric last week has not eroded the party's structural edge in this midterm cycle.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 28°C or below on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department forecasts project Karachi's maximum temperature on May 12 at around 35-36°C, reflecting trader consensus with 34.5% implied probability for 36°C and 33.5% for 35°C amid subsiding heatwave conditions following peaks above 40°C earlier in May. Coastal sea breezes, strengthening in recent days, have moderated highs to 34-36°C observed on May 10-11, countering high pressure over Sindh and elevated Arabian Sea surface temperatures that fuel pre-monsoon heat. Model ensembles show slight spread due to uncertain afternoon wind onset timing, which could suppress peaks below 36°C or allow urban heat island effects to push toward 37°C. Resolution hinges on PMD's official airport station reading, with final data expected May 13.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Sen. Jim Risch holds a commanding 96.8% implied probability in trader consensus for the Idaho Republican Senate primary on May 19, driven by his entrenched incumbency advantage, superior name recognition, and fundraising edge in the deep-red state. With ballots finalized last week and early voting underway in counties like Ada and Kootenai, recent coverage highlights low-profile challengers Joe Evans, Denny LaVé, and Josh Roy mounting criticism over Risch's age (83 on Election Day) and tenure, but absent public polls or momentum shifts. Historical patterns show Senate incumbents winning over 90% of primaries; upset scenarios remain slim absent a late scandal, health event, or high-profile endorsement reversal before polls close.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in custody following a February 2026 Seoul Central District Court life sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his December 2024 martial law declaration, upheld amid ongoing appeals. An April 29 appeals court ruling increased his separate seven-year term for obstructing arrest and bypassing cabinet processes, extending detention amid multiple charges including abuse of power. With trials continuing into mid-2026 and no bail grants or procedural releases since early detention extensions, trader consensus at 92.2% for "No" reflects judicial momentum toward prolonged incarceration, barring successful higher-court reversals or procedural breaks before 2027.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celtic's commanding 79% implied probability stems from their Scottish Premiership pedigree and recent 3-1 Old Firm victory over Rangers on May 10, extending a strong run including wins over Hibernian and Falkirk amid a tight title race where they sit second behind Hearts. Facing Championship fourth-placers Dunfermline at neutral Hampden Park in the Scottish Cup final, traders see vast quality gap despite the Pars' giantkilling path to the decider via triumphs over higher-tier sides like Hibs. Dunfermline's 20.5% upset chance and 21% draw reflect potential from returning striker Zak Rudden and defender Freddie Turley, while Celtic manages absences like Kasper Schmeichel's shoulder issue and Tomas Cvancara's recent groin blow with squad depth.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29 meeting, citing solid economic growth, low but steady job gains, and elevated inflation partly due to energy prices. April nonfarm payrolls increased by 115,000—below recent averages—with the unemployment rate holding at 4.3%, signaling labor market resilience amid cooling momentum. Polymarket traders reflect this balance, pricing a 49% implied probability on the rate hitting 3.5% before 2027 as the leading outcome, with low odds on hikes above 4.5%. Tomorrow's April CPI release, forecast at 3.7% year-over-year, and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting loom as pivotal catalysts for policy shifts.

Safi, 14th with 13 points from 19 games, sit in relegation peril yet showed resilience in a 1-1 draw at league leaders FAR Rabat four days ago, extending their winless run to six league outings (three draws) while scoring but conceding in each of their last nine Botola matches. Absent injury or suspension news, high draw probabilities reflect both sides' frequent stalemates and defensive vulnerabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just one month away starting June 11, trader consensus on nations reaching the final hinges on the December 2025 group draw and paths through the expanded 24-team knockout from the round of 32, favoring Spain (Group H with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay), France (Group I vs. Senegal, Iraq, Norway), and England (Group L vs. Croatia, Ghana, Panama) due to manageable group stages and opposite-bracket semifinal routes against Argentina or Brazil. Recent UEFA playoff triumphs in late March propelled Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye as potential dark horses, while mounting injuries—Rodrygo's ACL tear sidelining Brazil's depth, Germany's Serge Gnabry out with a torn adductor, and Spain's Lamine Yamal hamstring scare—introduce uncertainty amid final squad announcements. Host advantages boost USA (Group D), Mexico (Group A opener), and Canada (Group B), but no major developments have shifted standings in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

April Eurozone inflation surged to 3%, the highest since September 2023, prompting ECB caution after holding rates steady on April 30, while the Fed maintained its stance on April 29 amid resilient US data. This widens the interest rate differential favoring the dollar, though forecasts eye modest euro gains to 1.20-1.23 by year-end on anticipated one-to-two Fed cuts of 25 basis points. Key catalysts include the June 16-17 FOMC meeting and ECB's July 22-23 policy review, alongside May PMI releases. Polymarket odds aggregate real-money bets pricing in these macro dynamics and technical support around 1.1700.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder hold a commanding 3-0 lead over the Los Angeles Lakers in the Western Conference semifinals, with Game 4 set for tonight in Los Angeles, where no NBA playoff team has ever rallied from an 0-3 deficit. The Thunder's dominant 131-108 Game 3 blowout extended their playoff win streak to seven, fueled by depth from Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Chet Holmgren, and emerging contributors like Ajay Mitchell despite Jalen Williams remaining out with a left hamstring strain. Lakers face elimination without Luka Doncic (left hamstring), Jarred Vanderbilt questionable (finger dislocation), and persistent second-half collapses noted by coach JJ Redick, even after sweeping no regular-season meetings with OKC. Home-court advantage offers faint hope, but Thunder momentum drives trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit (LOW) $375 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Microsoft's share price hovers near $410, reflecting post-Q3 FY2026 earnings pressure despite an April 29 beat with $82.9 billion in revenue (up 18% year-over-year) and $4.27 adjusted EPS exceeding forecasts, fueled by Azure cloud and AI strength. Elevated $190 billion annual capex for AI infrastructure, alongside Kenyan data center delays and Xbox sales weakness, has spurred investor caution amid broader tech sector volatility. Analyst consensus price targets average $562, signaling long-term optimism on enterprise AI adoption and market leadership. Key near-term catalysts include the May 21 ex-dividend date ($0.91/share, 0.88% yield) and Q4 earnings on July 29, with traders monitoring for breakouts above recent highs around $420 or further downside toward the 52-week low of $356.

markets.question: FC Cincinnati vs. Inter Miami CF: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between FC Cincinnati and Inter Miami CF, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FC Cincinnati and Inter Miami CF combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Cincinnati vs. Inter Miami CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF holds third place in the MLS Eastern Conference with 12 points from seven matches (3-1-3 record), fueled by Lionel Messi's sharp form after shaking off a February preseason hamstring strain, as confirmed in recent recovery sessions and lineups against Austin FC. FC Cincinnati, sitting mid-table after a gritty 1-1 draw at Toronto FC on April 11—where center-back Matt Miazga exited with leg discomfort—seeks to capitalize on home-field edge at TQL Stadium for the May 13 clash. Head-to-head history is evenly split (Cincinnati 5 wins, Miami 6, 3 draws in 14 MLS meetings), with Miami's attack facing Cincinnati's stout defense amid lingering concerns like Evander's foot injury recovery and potential Miazga absence. Traders eye Messi's influence, recent momentum, and injury updates as pivotal swings.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Tokyo be 22°C or higher on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight probabilities across 15–18°C outcomes reflect genuine uncertainty in Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and global forecast models for Tokyo's overnight low on May 13, driven by divergent projections on cloud cover and light winds influencing radiative cooling. Current JMA guidance indicates overcast conditions with southerly breezes, favoring minimal cooling to 16–18°C amid persistent urban heat island effects that typically elevate central Tokyo readings by 1–2°C above rural areas; however, ECMWF ensembles suggest intermittent clearing could allow dips toward 15°C, aligning with recent early-May nights averaging 14–17°C and climatological norms of 15–16°C. Key differentiators include exact timing of minimum temperature (pre-dawn hours) and moisture levels; traders eye JMA's twice-daily updates on May 12 for three-hourly refinements resolving model spreads.

markets.question: Will the 2028 Democratic Presidential nominee be a woman?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the individual that wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president is a woman. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.
